model_narrative: We route vehicle platoons across a city traffic network over a sequence of time periods. The network is expanded in time so that crossing a physical link means choosing both the link and how long the crossing takes, which fixes the departure period and the arrival period. For each such time-expanded crossing we decide whether to use it at all, and how much traffic headed to each final destination to send along it. The objective is to minimize the total travel effort across all crossings plus the trip-completion penalties incurred by flows that finish their journeys at the horizon end.

sets.name: N
sets.doc: the nodes of the traffic network, indexed by integers

sets.name: A
sets.doc: the directed physical links of the network, each an ordered pair of an origin node and a destination node

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the discrete time periods of the planning horizon in chronological order; the last member is the final period (the horizon end)

sets.name: cal_A
sets.doc: the set of time-expanded links; each member is a four-tuple giving an origin node, a departure period, a destination node, and an arrival period, where the origin and destination form a physical link and the departure period is earlier than the arrival period; the gap between departure and arrival is the travel time chosen for that crossing

params.name: M
params.doc: a single large constant used to switch link inequalities on or off depending on whether a time-expanded link is selected; a scalar with value 5000

params.name: c
params.doc: the carrying capacity of each time-expanded link, in vehicles; a number computed for each origin, departure period, destination, and arrival period that reflects how much flow that particular crossing duration can hold

params.name: beta
params.doc: the trip-completion penalty for each origin and destination node pair

params.name: nu
params.doc: the travel demand entering the network at an origin node for a given destination node in a given period, in vehicles

vars.name: delta
vars.doc: selection indicator for a time-expanded link; equals 1 if that crossing (a given origin, departure period, destination, and arrival period) is used and 0 otherwise

vars.name: f_d
vars.doc: the traffic volume routed on a time-expanded link that is bound for a particular final destination node; indexed by the origin, departure period, destination, and arrival period of the crossing together with the destination node the flow is headed to, in vehicles

constraints.name: f_d[x,
constraints.intent: Traffic can only travel on a crossing that has actually been selected, and a selected crossing can carry only a limited amount of traffic. For each time-expanded crossing, the total traffic sent along it across all final destinations must stay within a large allowance when the crossing is used, and must drop to nothing when the crossing is not used.

constraints.name: S)
constraints.intent: Once vehicles leave a node along a physical link in a given period, they cannot spread out into more than one crossing of that link. For each origin node, each neighboring node it links to, and each period before the horizon end, at most one crossing of that link starting in that period may be selected across all possible arrival times.

constraints.name: S)
constraints.intent: Vehicles that enter a link earlier must not arrive later than vehicles that enter the same link afterward, so crossings of one link keep their order in time. For each origin node, each neighboring node it links to, and each pair of departure periods where the first is earlier than the second and the second is before the horizon end, the arrival period of the earlier departure must be no later than the arrival period of the later departure. This ordering is enforced only when the later departure actually has a selected crossing, and is otherwise relaxed so it does not bind.

constraints.name: S)
constraints.intent: The traffic occupying a link at a given moment cannot exceed the capacity of the crossing being used. For each origin node, each neighboring node it links to, and each period before the horizon end, add up all traffic that is in transit on that link at that period, counting flows that departed in that period or earlier and arrive in that period or later, across all final destinations. This total must not exceed the capacity of the selected crossing that starts in that period, with a large allowance added when no such crossing is selected so the limit does not bind.

query: The network team is floating a leaner plan that activates fewer scheduled links. We're looking at holding the total number of active time-expanded links to no more than 38. What does the optimizer return for total travel time?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_links = Constraint(expr=sum(model.delta[k] for k in model.delta) <= 38)

query: There's a proposal on the table to throttle the link out of node 4 at the start that reaches node 2 by period 3 — keep its total traffic at or below 20 vehicles. Plug that in and tell me the resulting travel time.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_arc4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.f_d[4, 0, 2, 3, d] for d in model.N) <= 20)

query: Suppose node 2 has a hard receiving limit right at the end of the window — total traffic arriving into node 2 exactly at the final period can't exceed 80 vehicles. What does the optimizer return for travel time under that cap?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_in2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.f_d[x, tx, 2, 5, d] for (x, tx, y, ty) in model.cal_A if y == 2 and ty == 5 for d in model.N) <= 80)

query: Engineering is pitching a single-departure rule at the source: node 1 may activate at most one outbound link at the very start of the horizon. Run it and walk me through the travel-time result.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_out1 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.delta[1, 0, y, ty] for (x, tx, y, ty) in model.cal_A if x == 1 and tx == 0) <= 1)

query: Node 4 looks like a bottleneck the way everything funnels through it. Wouldn't it be cleaner to hold the number of active links arriving into node 4 to eight at most? What's the model seeing that I'm not?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_into4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.delta[x, tx, y, ty] for (x, tx, y, ty) in model.cal_A if y == 4) <= 8)

query: The corridor from node 1 to node 2 carries a huge share of the total traffic across the horizon, and that worries me. Couldn't we hold the combined flow on the node-1-to-node-2 links to 200 vehicles and spread the load? Walk me through what's pulling the plan the other way.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_12 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.f_d[1, tx, 2, ty, d] for (xx, tx, yy, ty) in model.cal_A if xx == 1 and yy == 2 for d in model.N) <= 200)

query: We seem to be switching on a lot of links across the schedule. Is there a reason the plan can't run on 36 active links or fewer and still get everyone where they're going? What's the tradeoff I'm missing?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_links_low = Constraint(expr=sum(model.delta[k] for k in model.delta) <= 36)
